children.heading: Agricultural commodity
children.label: 1 Agricultural commodity
children.text: The term “agricultural commodity” means any agricultural commodity, food, feed, fiber, or livestock that is subject to a covered program.

children.label: i
children.text: a program administered by the Secretary under which price or income support, or production or market loss assistance, is provided to producers of agricultural commodities; and

children.label: ii
children.text: a conservation program administered by the Secretary.

children.label: i
children.text: an agricultural credit program carried out under the Consolidated Farm and Rural Development Act ( et seq.); or7 U.S.C. 1921

children.label: ii
children.text: the crop insurance program carried out under the Federal Crop Insurance Act ( et seq.).7 U.S.C. 1501

children.heading: Participant
children.label: 3 Participant
children.text: The term “participant” means a participant in a covered program.

children.heading: State Conservationist
children.label: 4 State Conservationist
children.text: The term “State Conservationist” means the State Conservationist with respect to a program administered by the Natural Resources Conservation Service.

children.heading: State Director
children.label: 5 State Director
children.text: The term “State Director” means the State Executive Director of the Farm Service Agency with respect to a program administered by the Farm Service Agency.
acting in good faith, relied on the action or advice of the Secretary (including any authorized representative of the Secretary) to the detriment of the participant; or
failed to comply fully with the requirements of the covered program, but made a good faith effort to comply with the requirements.
retain loans, payments, or other benefits received under the covered program;
continue to receive loans, payments, and other benefits under the covered program;
continue to participate, in whole or in part, under any contract executed under the covered program;
in the case of a conservation program, reenroll all or part of the land covered by the program; and
receive such other equitable relief as the Secretary determines to be appropriate.

children.heading: Remedial action
children.label: d Remedial action
children.text: As a condition of receiving relief under this section, the Secretary may require the participant to take actions designed to remedy any failure to comply with the covered program.

children.heading: Equitable relief by State Directors and State Conservationists
children.label: e Equitable relief by State Directors and State Conservationists
A
children.text: the amount of loans, payments, and benefits for which relief will be provided to the participant under this subsection is less than $20,000;

children.label: B
children.text: the total amount of loans, payments, and benefits for which relief has been previously provided to the participant under this subsection is not more than $5,000; and

children.label: C
children.text: the total amount of loans, payments, and benefits for which relief is provided to similarly situated participants under this subsection is not more than $1,000,000, as determined by the Secretary.

children.label: A
children.text: shall not require prior approval by the Administrator of the Farm Service Agency, the Chief of the Natural Resources Conservation Service, or any other officer or employee of the Agency or Service;

children.label: B
children.text: shall be made only after consultation with, and the approval of, the Office of General Counsel of the Department of Agriculture; and

children.label: C
children.text: is subject to reversal only by the Secretary (who may not delegate the reversal authority).

children.label: i
children.text: sections 1001 through 1001F of the Food Security Act of 1985 ( et seq.); or7 U.S.C. 1308

children.label: ii
children.text: a conservation program administered by the Secretary.

children.label: B
children.text: highly erodible land and wetland conservation requirements under subtitle B or C of title XII of the Food Security Act of 1985 ( et seq.).16 U.S.C. 3811

children.heading: Other authority
children.label: 4 Other authority
children.text: The authority provided to a State Director and State Conservationist under this subsection is in addition to any other applicable authority and does not limit other authority provided by law or the Secretary.

children.heading: Judicial review
children.label: f Judicial review
children.text: A discretionary decision by the Secretary, the State Director, or the State Conservationist under this section shall be final, and shall not be subject to review under chapter 7 of title 5.
the number of requests for equitable relief under subsections (b) and (e) and the disposition of the requests; and
the number of requests for equitable relief under and the disposition of the requests.section 6998(d) of this title

children.heading: Relationship to other law
children.label: h Relationship to other law
children.text: The authority provided in this section is in addition to any other authority provided in this or any other Act.
